metadata.summary: Catalog index of software-developer methodologies by language and domain. Routing map for finding the right methodology subfolder by topic.

content.title: Software Developer Files Reference
content.summary: Catalog index of all methodology folders in the `software-developer` skill, grouped by language and domain (Python, Django, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, Rust, Ruby, PHP, Java, C#, API Design, Testing, Frontend, Architecture, Dev Practices). Used as a routing map: agent reads the topical section, picks the slug, then resolves the actual `[slug]/methodology.xml` path via `Glob`. The list predates the unified-XML restructure and may be stale — always verify existence with a filesystem lookup before loading a path.

why.p: The `software-developer` skill contains 200+ methodology entries across many languages. A grouped catalog lets an agent identify the topical section (e.g. "Go") in one read, then resolve the actual subfolder path via `Glob`, rather than scanning the entire tree. Faster routing, lower context cost, fewer hallucinated paths.

when-to-use.p: Apply this catalog when an agent inside the `software-developer` orchestrator needs to map a free-text task ("Set up pytest fixtures", "Write a Gin handler") to a single methodology folder before loading content. Use only as a first-pass routing index — never as final source-of-truth.

list.item: Routing step inside `software-developer` orchestrator: pick the topical section, then the slug.
Building a documentation index or sidebar from the canonical catalog.
Validating that referenced methodology folders exist before dispatching a task to a sub-agent.
As a semantic search tool ("how do I handle errors?") — the list is name-based; use `Grep` over actual `methodology.xml` bodies.
As an authoritative tier map — some entries listed here belong logically to other tiers; trust the directory tree at `skills/faion/knowledge/<tier>/<group>/<domain>/<slug>/`.
Dumping the full catalog into a sub-agent prompt — it is too large; filter to one topical section only.

how-to.step: 1. Read this file once and locate the topical `<section>` whose name matches the task language or area (e.g. `python`, `go`, `testing`).
2. From the matching section, pick at most one or two slugs whose name best fits the task verb + noun.
3. Resolve each candidate path with `Glob 'skills/faion/knowledge/**/{slug}/methodology.xml'`. If the unified file is missing, fall back to `Glob 'skills/faion/knowledge/**/{slug}/README.md'`.
4. If neither exists, run `Grep -ri "<title>.*{slug-keyword}" skills/faion/knowledge` to discover the renamed folder. Never invent a path.
5. Read only the resolved file. Pass the absolute path (not the catalog snippet) to the downstream agent.
6. If the user asked for "all X methodologies", list resolved paths only — never paste catalog rows that failed step 3.

checklist.check: Picked exactly one topical section (not the whole catalog).
Each candidate slug came verbatim from this file (no invented suffixes).
Each path was verified with `Glob` or `ls` before loading.
Missing paths reported as "not found" — never fabricated.
Final agent prompt contains resolved absolute paths, not catalog text.
No edits made to this catalog from an automated step (human-only).

content.prompt:
You are the routing step for the software-developer skill.
INPUT: task = "<one-line task description from the user>"
CATALOG: <paste only the matching <section> block from files-reference/methodology.xml>
INSTRUCTIONS: 1. From CATALOG, pick at most TWO slugs whose name best matches the task verb + noun. 2. For each picked slug, return: - slug - candidate_path = "skills/faion/knowledge/free/dev/software-developer/<slug>/methodology.xml" - confidence = high|medium|low 3. Do NOT load or summarize file contents. Do NOT invent slugs. 4. Output strict JSON: { "candidates": [ { "slug": "...", "candidate_path": "...", "confidence": "..." } ] } 5. The orchestrator will Glob each candidate_path before loading.

verify.step: 1. Run the validator: `python3 scripts/validate-methodology-xml.py skills/faion/knowledge/free/dev/software-developer/files-reference/methodology.xml`. Expect `PASS` and exit code 0.
2. Spot-check three random slugs from different sections with `Glob skills/faion/knowledge/**/{slug}/methodology.xml`. Each should resolve to exactly one path or be reported as missing.
3. Confirm the agent's downstream prompt contains absolute resolved paths, not raw catalog rows.
4. If >20% of spot-checked slugs are missing, file an issue under `.aidocs/backlog/` for a catalog refresh — do not auto-edit.

list.item: django-decision-tree — Decision tree for Django patterns

section.name: usage-pattern
section.p: Treat this catalog as a read-only routing index at agent runtime — do not have agents append to it; updates must come from a human-driven restructure pass. When adding a new methodology folder, also add a row here in the matching topical section so future routing finds it. Prefer `[slug]/methodology.xml` lookups over flat-file lookups; the migration to unified files is the long-term direction. For routing prompts, include only the topical section the task hits — the full catalog costs context for little gain.

Agent workflow for routing: Step 1 read this `files-reference/methodology.xml` and identify the topical group (Python / Django / Go / ...); Step 2 `Glob skills/faion/knowledge/**/<slug>/methodology.xml`; Step 3 if missing, fall back to `Grep` across methodology files for the slug; never quote the catalog wholesale into the prompt — only the resolved path. Audit task: walk every link in this catalog. For each path: check existence (as unified `methodology.xml`). Output a table of `{referenced_path, resolved_path, status}`. Do not fix anything — report only.

section.name: agent-gotchas
section.p: The list is name-based; methodology files are now unified XML, not flat Markdown. Don't promise a user based on old line counts from this index. Don't auto-Read every file the user asks about based on this index alone — many entries may be missing. Always `Glob` first to verify presence. An agent asked to "list all Go files" will paste this section verbatim; remind it to verify existence to avoid hallucinated capabilities. LLMs sometimes "fix" broken links by inventing nearby paths. Add a human-in-loop checkpoint before any commit that mutates this file. This index is purely a catalog, not a methodology — agents looking for checklist content or templates will find empty boilerplate here; route them to the actual methodology folder instead.
